## Summary extracted EPS items

| Fiscal year | Prior-year adjusted diluted EPS base | Initial full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook | Implied initial EPS range* | Actual fiscal-year adjusted diluted EPS | Later FY2025 update |
|---|---:|---|---:|---:|---|
| FY2023 | $3.94 (FY2022) | Flat to up 3% in constant currency from $3.94 | ~$3.94–$4.06 | $4.30 | — |
| FY2024 | $4.30 (FY2023) | Up 4% to 6% in constant currency from $4.30 | ~$4.47–$4.56 | $4.52 | — |
| FY2025 | $4.52 (FY2024) | Down 1% to up 1% in constant currency from $4.52 | ~$4.47–$4.57 | Not yet available in provided docs | Updated to down 8% to down 7% in constant currency, implied ~$4.16–$4.20 vs $4.52 base |

\*Implied EPS ranges are calculated from the quoted percentage outlook and quoted base EPS, rounded to the nearest cent. The company’s outlook is stated as constant-currency growth, not as a formal dollar EPS range.

---

## FY2023

### FY2022 actual / FY2023 outlook release — issued June 29, 2022

**FY2022 actual adjusted diluted EPS / base for FY2023 outlook**

> “Diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $4.42 were up 17 percent; adjusted diluted EPS of $3.94 increased 4 percent in constant currency.”

**FY2023 full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook**

> “Adjusted diluted EPS are expected to range between flat and up 3 percent in constant currency from the base of $3.94 earned in fiscal 2022, including a 3-point net headwind from divestitures and acquisitions announced or closed in fiscal 2022.”

### FY2023 results / FY2024 outlook release — issued June 28, 2023

**FY2023 actual adjusted diluted EPS**

> “Diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $4.31 was down 2 percent from the prior year; adjusted diluted EPS of $4.30 was up 10 percent in constant currency”

**FY2023 actual and FY2022 prior-year adjusted diluted EPS from reconciliation table**

> “Adjusted diluted earnings per share  
> $ 4.30  
> $ 3.94  
> 9 %”

> “Adjusted diluted earnings per share growth, on a constant-currency basis  
> 10 %”

---

## FY2024

### FY2023 results / FY2024 outlook release — issued June 28, 2023

**FY2023 actual adjusted diluted EPS / base for FY2024 outlook**

> “Adjusted diluted EPS of $4.30 was up 10 percent in constant currency, driven primarily by higher adjusted operating profit, a lower adjusted effective tax rate, and lower net shares outstanding, partially offset by lower benefit plan non-service income and lower after-tax earnings from joint ventures.”

**FY2024 full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook**

> “Adjusted diluted EPS is expected to increase 4 to 6 percent in constant currency from the base of $4.30 earned in fiscal 2023.”

### FY2024 results / FY2025 outlook release — issued June 26, 2024

**FY2024 actual adjusted diluted EPS**

> “Diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $4.31 essentially matched year-ago results; adjusted diluted EPS of $4.52 was up 6 percent in constant currency”

**FY2024 actual and FY2023 prior-year adjusted diluted EPS from reconciliation table**

> “Adjusted diluted earnings per share  
> $ 4.52  
> $ 4.30  
> 5 %”

> “Adjusted diluted earnings per share growth, on a constant-currency basis  
> 6 %”

---

## FY2025

### FY2024 results / FY2025 outlook release — issued June 26, 2024

**FY2024 actual adjusted diluted EPS / base for FY2025 outlook**

> “Adjusted diluted EPS of $4.52 was up 6 percent in constant currency, driven primarily by higher adjusted operating profit and lower net shares outstanding, partially offset by higher net interest expense and lower benefit plan non-service income.”

**Initial FY2025 full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook**

> “Adjusted diluted EPS is expected to range between down 1 percent and up 1 percent in constant currency from the base of $4.52 earned in fiscal 2024.”

### FY2025 Q3 update — issued March 19, 2025

**Updated FY2025 full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook**

> “Adjusted operating profit and adjusted diluted EPS are now expected to be down 8 percent to down 7 percent in constant currency, compared to the previous ranges of between down 4 percent and down 2 percent in constant currency, reflecting lower net sales.”

**Relevant FY2025 year-to-date adjusted diluted EPS context**

> “Adjusted diluted EPS of $3.47 was down 1 percent in constant currency, driven primarily by lower adjusted operating profit, higher net interest expense, and a higher adjusted effective tax rate, partially offset by lower net shares outstanding.”

I treated the request as covering the three named documents only: **FY2023 results/outlook**, **FY2024 results/outlook**, and **FY2024 Q3**. I did **not** extract from the FY2025 Q3 document that was also pasted.

## FY2023 results/outlook document

**Date/title:** **June 28, 2023 — “General Mills Reports Fiscal 2023 Fourth-quarter and Full-year Results and Provides Fiscal 2024 Outlook”**

### Actual adjusted diluted EPS passages

- **Full Year Highlights:** “Diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $4.31 was down 2 percent from the prior year; **adjusted diluted EPS of $4.30 was up 10 percent in constant currency**”

- **Fourth Quarter Highlights:** “Diluted EPS of $1.03 was down 24 percent; **adjusted diluted EPS of $1.12 increased 1 percent in constant currency**”

- “We delivered excellent results in fiscal 2023, including generating double-digit growth in organic net sales and **constant-currency adjusted diluted EPS** and exceeding $20 billion in annual net sales for the first time in our company’s history,” said General Mills Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Jeff Harmening.

- **Fourth Quarter Results Summary:** “Net earnings attributable to General Mills of $615 million were down 25 percent and diluted EPS was down 24 percent to $1.03, driven primarily by lower operating profit. **Adjusted diluted EPS of $1.12 increased 1 percent in constant currency**, driven primarily by lower net shares outstanding, partially offset by lower benefit plan non-service income.”

- **Full Year Results Summary:** “Net earnings attributable to General Mills were down 4 percent to $2.6 billion and diluted EPS was down 2 percent to $4.31, primarily reflecting lower operating profit, a higher effective tax rate, and lower after-tax earnings from joint ventures, partially offset by lower net shares outstanding. **Adjusted diluted EPS of $4.30 was up 10 percent in constant currency**, driven primarily by higher adjusted operating profit, a lower adjusted effective tax rate, and lower net shares outstanding, partially offset by lower benefit plan non-service income and lower after-tax earnings from joint ventures.”

- **Adjusted Diluted EPS and Related Constant-currency Growth Rate table:**  
  “Adjusted diluted earnings per share — Quarter Ended May 28, 2023: **$1.12**; May 29, 2022: $1.12; Change: Flat. Fiscal Year 2023: **$4.30**; Fiscal Year 2022: $3.94; Change: 9%. Foreign currency exchange impact: (1)pt. **Adjusted diluted earnings per share growth, on a constant-currency basis: Quarter 1%; Fiscal Year 10%.**”

### Full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook/guidance passages

- **Fiscal 2024 Outlook:** “With these assumptions in mind, General Mills outlined its full-year financial targets for fiscal 2024²:”

- “**Adjusted diluted EPS is expected to increase 4 to 6 percent in constant currency from the base of $4.30 earned in fiscal 2023.**”

- “The net impact of divestitures and foreign currency exchange is expected to reduce full-year reported net sales growth by approximately one half of one percent, and foreign currency exchange is expected to have an immaterial impact on adjusted operating profit and **adjusted diluted EPS growth**.”

- **Note 7:** “Our fiscal 2024 outlook for organic net sales growth, constant-currency adjusted operating profit, **adjusted diluted EPS**, and free cash flow conversion are non-GAAP financial measures that exclude, or have otherwise been adjusted for, items impacting comparability, including the effect of foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, restructuring charges and project-related costs, acquisition transaction and integration costs, acquisitions, divestitures, and mark-to-market effects.”

---

## FY2024 results/outlook document

**Date/title:** **June 26, 2024 — “General Mills Reports Fiscal 2024 Fourth-quarter and Full-year Results and Provides Fiscal 2025 Outlook”**

### Actual adjusted diluted EPS passages

- **Full Year Highlights:** “Diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $4.31 essentially matched year-ago results; **adjusted diluted EPS of $4.52 was up 6 percent in constant currency**”

- **Fourth Quarter Highlights:** “Diluted EPS of $0.98 was down 5 percent; **adjusted diluted EPS of $1.01 was down 10 percent in constant currency**”

- **Fourth Quarter Results Summary:** “Net earnings attributable to General Mills of $558 million were down 9 percent. Diluted EPS was down 5 percent to $0.98, driven primarily by lower operating profit, higher net interest expense, and a higher effective tax rate, partially offset by lower net shares outstanding. **Adjusted diluted EPS of $1.01 was down 10 percent in constant currency**, driven primarily by lower adjusted operating profit, higher net interest expense, and a higher adjusted effective tax rate, partially offset by lower net shares outstanding.”

- **Full Year Results Summary:** “Net earnings attributable to General Mills were down 4 percent to $2.5 billion. Diluted EPS of $4.31 essentially matched year-ago results, with lower net shares outstanding offset by higher net interest expense and lower benefit plan non-service income. **Adjusted diluted EPS of $4.52 was up 6 percent in constant currency**, driven primarily by higher adjusted operating profit and lower net shares outstanding, partially offset by higher net interest expense and lower benefit plan non-service income.”

- **Adjusted Diluted EPS and Related Constant-currency Growth Rate table:**  
  “Adjusted diluted earnings per share — Quarter Ended May 26, 2024: **$1.01**; May 28, 2023: $1.12; Change: (10)%. Fiscal Year 2024: **$4.52**; Fiscal Year 2023: $4.30; Change: 5%. Foreign currency exchange impact: Flat. **Adjusted diluted earnings per share growth, on a constant-currency basis: Quarter (10)%; Fiscal Year 6%.**”

### Full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook/guidance passages

- **Fiscal 2025 Outlook:** “With these assumptions in mind, General Mills outlined its full-year financial targets for fiscal 2025²:”

- “**Adjusted diluted EPS is expected to range between down 1 percent and up 1 percent in constant currency from the base of $4.52 earned in fiscal 2024.**”

- **Note 7:** “Our fiscal 2025 outlook for organic net sales growth, constant-currency adjusted operating profit, **adjusted diluted EPS**, and free cash flow conversion are non-GAAP financial measures that exclude, or have otherwise been adjusted for, items impacting comparability, including the effect of foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, restructuring charges, acquisition transaction and integration costs, acquisitions, divestitures, and mark-to-market effects.”

---

## FY2024 Q3 document

**Date/title:** **Mar. 20, 2024 — “General Mills Reports Fiscal 2024 Third-quarter Results”**

### Actual adjusted diluted EPS passages

- **Opening bullets:** “Diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.17 were up 27 percent; **adjusted diluted EPS of $1.17 increased 22 percent in constant currency**”

- **Third Quarter Results Summary:** “Net earnings attributable to General Mills of $670 million were up 21 percent. Diluted EPS was up 27 percent to $1.17, driven primarily by higher operating profit and lower net shares outstanding, partially offset by higher net interest expense and a higher effective tax rate. **Adjusted diluted EPS of $1.17 was up 22 percent in constant currency**, driven primarily by higher adjusted operating profit, lower net shares outstanding, and a lower adjusted effective tax rate, partially offset by higher net interest expense.”

- **Nine Month Results Summary:** “Net earnings attributable to General Mills were down 2 percent to $1.9 billion. Diluted EPS was up 2 percent to $3.33, driven primarily by lower net shares outstanding and higher operating profit, partially offset by higher net interest expense. **Adjusted diluted EPS of $3.51 was up 11 percent in constant currency**, driven primarily by higher adjusted operating profit, lower net shares outstanding, and a lower adjusted effective tax rate, partially offset by higher net interest expense.”

- **Adjusted Diluted EPS and Related Constant-currency Growth Rates table:**  
  “Adjusted diluted earnings per share — Quarter Ended Feb. 25, 2024: **$1.17**; Feb. 26, 2023: $0.97; Change: 21%. Nine-Month Period Ended Feb. 25, 2024: **$3.51**; Feb. 26, 2023: $3.18; Change: 10%. Foreign currency exchange impact: (1)pt. **Adjusted diluted earnings per share growth, on a constant-currency basis: Quarter 22%; Nine-Month Period 11%.**”

### Full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook/guidance passages

- **Opening bullets:** “Company reaffirms full-year fiscal 2024 outlook”

- **Fiscal 2024 Outlook:** “General Mills continues to expect the largest factors impacting its performance in fiscal 2024 will be the economic health of consumers, the moderating rate of input cost inflation, and the increasing stability of the supply chain environment. Based on its current outlook on these and other key factors, the company reaffirmed its full-year fiscal 2024 financial targets²:”

- “**Adjusted operating profit and adjusted diluted EPS are each expected to increase 4 to 5 percent in constant currency.**”

- “The net impact of divestitures and foreign currency exchange is expected to have an immaterial impact on full-year reported net sales growth, and foreign currency exchange is expected to have an immaterial impact on adjusted operating profit and **adjusted diluted EPS growth**.”

- **Note 7:** “Our fiscal 2024 outlook for organic net sales growth, adjusted operating profit growth, **adjusted diluted EPS growth**, and free cash flow conversion are non-GAAP financial measures that exclude, or have otherwise been adjusted for, items impacting comparability, including the effect of foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, acquisitions, divestitures, and a 53rd week, when applicable.”

steps.result:
**Date/Title:** **Mar. 23, 2023 — “General Mills Reports Fiscal 2023 Third-quarter Results and Raises Full-year Outlook”**

**Fiscal 2023 full-year adjusted diluted EPS outlook/guidance passage:**

> “With stronger and more broad-based business momentum, the company is raising its outlook for fiscal 2023. The updated full-year financial targets are summarized below¹:  
> …  
> **Adjusted diluted EPS is now expected to increase 8 to 9 percent in constant currency, compared to the previous range of up 7 to 8 percent.** Both the current and previous ranges include a **3-point net headwind from divestitures and acquisitions** and an estimated **1-point headwind from the ice cream recall**.”
